etymology.description: semantic 亻(人) (rén, "person") + phonetic 左(左) (zuǒ) -> to assist
etymology.interpretation: The character 佐(佐) (zuǒ, "to assist") combines 亻(人) (rén, "person"), indicating action related to a person, with 左(左) (zuǒ), which provides the sound. The idea is of a person providing support or assistance, aligning with the meaning of 'to assist'.

examples.chinese: 他在工作上佐我(他在工作上佐我)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài gōngzuò shàng zuǒ wǒ.

examples.chinese: 这位年轻的医生通过佐导师获得了宝贵的经验(這位年輕的醫生通過佐導師獲得了寶貴的經驗)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè wèi niánqīng de yīshēng tōngguò zuǒ dǎoshī huòdéle bǎoguì de jīngyàn.
examples.english: The young doctor gained valuable experience through assisting the mentor.

examples.chinese: 他愿意佐国家渡过难关(他願意佐國家渡過難關)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yuànyì zuǒ guójiā dùguò nánguān.
examples.english: He is willing to assist the country through difficult times.
